query:
INSERT INTO public.questions (
    id, question_text, question_type, options, correct_answer, explanation, hint,
    stimulus_text, stimulus_type, section, topic, difficulty, exam_type,
    is_platform_question, ai_generated, calculator_allowed, subject, subtopic,
    math_domain, pool_key, diagnostic_eligible, tags, external_id, source, source_metadata
  ) VALUES
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this argument, the author’s conclusion that many people in that country will not be able to make informed decisions about the food they consume unless a solution is found to address this issue relies on what assumption?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"people in that country are likely to eat more fresh produce in the future.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"people in that country are likely to eat less packaged food in the future.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"most people in that country are not able to make informed decisions about the food they consume.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"the nutritional information that is found on packaged food is accurate.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The author''s argument is based on the assumption that "most people in that country are not able to make informed decisions about the food they consume." The author believes that people are unable to make informed decisions because they can’t read the small print on food labels.  This argument relies on the assumption that people who can’t read the small print on food labels are unable to make informed decisions about the food they consume. If people in that country can make informed decisions about the food they consume even if they cannot read the small print on food labels, the author’s argument falls apart.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author of the text states that "the vast majority" of people who use their cell phones in a certain country are not able to read the small print on food labels.  The author also states that many people in that country are beginning to eat more fresh produce, including produce that is not labeled with the nutritional information that is found on packaged food.  The author concludes that many people in that country will not be able to make informed decisions about the food they consume unless a solution is found to address this issue.  The author’s argument relies on the assumption that',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_b6',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The text suggests that the positive effects of meditation on well-being may be due to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"the ability of meditation to reduce stress and anxiety.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"the ability of meditation to improve focus and concentration.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"the ability of meditation to increase blood flow to the brain.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"the ability of meditation to change the structure of the brain.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The text states that the study found that meditation led to changes in the brain''s structure. This finding supports the text’s claim that meditation can have a positive effect on well-being by suggesting that changes in the brain’s structure are associated with positive effects on well-being. The other choices are not directly supported by the text.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The text describes a study of the effects of meditation on the brain. According to the text, the scientists who conducted the study found that meditation led to a change in the brain''s structure. How does this finding support the text’s claim that meditation can have a positive effect on well-being?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_c6',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author of this text highlights the many factors that influence communication in order to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"explain why communication is a complex process.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"suggest that communication is becoming more difficult.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"emphasize the importance of technology in communication.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"argue that communication is not always effective.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The author highlights the different factors that influence communication to emphasize the complexity of the process.  By doing so, the author suggests that there is no one-size-fits-all approach to communication.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'Throughout history, people have used a variety of methods to communicate with one another. In the early days, people relied on spoken language, gestures, and visual signals.  Later, the invention of writing provided a more permanent means of communication.  With the advent of the telegraph, telephone, and the internet, communication became faster and more efficient.  In addition to these technological advances, the development of new languages and dialects has also played a role in shaping the way we communicate.  It is important to remember that communication is a complex process that involves many different factors, and there is no one-size-fits-all approach that is right for everyone.  What is the most likely reason why the author of the text highlights the different factors that influence communication?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_a1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The most recent studies indicate that the benefits of being bilingual extend far beyond improved communication skills. Not only does learning a second language make it easier to acquire new skills, ______ it also improves overall cognitive ability.',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"for example,","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"in contrast,","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"however,","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"furthermore,","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The sentence uses a transition to highlight the additional benefit of being bilingual, making "furthermore" the most appropriate choice. This transition clarifies the relationship between learning a second language and overall cognitive ability.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    NULL,
    NULL,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '54c1b2dd',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author''s inclusion of the phrase "challenge the dominant narratives" in the passage most likely serves to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"suggest that the author is critical of the current state of media representation.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"highlight the importance of creating a more inclusive media landscape.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"provide a specific example of how to achieve greater diversity in the media.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"emphasize the need for a more nuanced understanding of the world.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The phrase "challenge the dominant narratives" implies that the author believes the current media landscape is lacking in diverse perspectives and that these dominant narratives need to be challenged in order to create a more accurate and representative representation of the world.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In a recent article discussing the need for greater diversity in the media, the author argues that: "It is important to not only represent different voices but also to challenge the dominant narratives that have shaped our understanding of the world." What is the most likely reason why the author includes the phrase "challenge the dominant narratives"?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Expression of Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Expression of Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Expression of Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_c7',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Expression of Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best summarizes the author’s main idea?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Genetic engineering has the potential to benefit society, but scientists must be careful not to use it in ways that are harmful or controversial.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The development of new biofuels and biodegradable plastics has increased public confidence in the safety of genetic engineering.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The public''s distrust of genetic engineering is unfounded, because genetic engineering has the potential to lead to many breakthroughs in medicine.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Scientists are working to develop new ways to use genetic engineering in medicine that are not controversial.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The author argues that while genetic engineering has the potential to improve society, scientists must proceed cautiously to avoid harmful or controversial applications of the technology.  The text mentions that the public''s distrust of genetic engineering is rooted in the potential risks of the technology.  The author ends by describing alternative applications, which are less controversial. These points suggest that the author''s main idea is that while genetic engineering has the potential to benefit society, scientists must be careful not to use it in ways that are harmful or controversial.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In the 1970s, the first commercial production of genetically engineered bacteria was developed for use in medicine.  However, the public’s distrust of the new technology has led to a growing awareness of the potential risks of genetic engineering.  As a result, scientists are now focusing on safer and less controversial applications of this technology, such as developing new biofuels and biodegradable plastics.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_e12',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'What is the main idea of this passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Smart dust particles could be used to monitor the environment in ways that have never before been possible.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Smart dust particles are very small, and each one contains sensors that can measure things like temperature, humidity, and air quality.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The author believes that smart dust could be used for a variety of applications, such as tracking pollution levels, monitoring the health of crops, and even detecting early signs of earthquakes.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The author believes that smart dust is the best way to monitor the environment","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The main idea of the passage is that smart dust particles can be used to monitor the environment in ways that have never before been possible.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The article describes how a new type of technology, called "smart dust," could be used to monitor the environment in ways that have never before been possible. Smart dust particles are very small, and each one contains sensors that can measure things like temperature, humidity, and air quality. The particles are also equipped with wireless communication capabilities, so they can send data back to a central location for analysis.  The author believes that smart dust could be used for a variety of applications, such as tracking pollution levels, monitoring the health of crops, and even detecting early signs of earthquakes.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    '213a45cf',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best combines the sentences at the underlined portion so that they are grammatically correct and maintain the meaning of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"This equation shows that the process of photosynthesis, which plants use to create their own food, results in the production of sugar and oxygen, while the sugar is used by the plant for growth and energy.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"This equation shows that the process of photosynthesis, which plants use to create their own food, results in the production of sugar and oxygen; while the sugar is used by the plant for growth and energy.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"This equation shows that the process of photosynthesis, which plants use to create their own food, results in the production of sugar and oxygen; the sugar is used by the plant for growth and energy.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"This equation shows that the process of photosynthesis, which plants use to create their own food, results in the production of sugar and oxygen, and the sugar is used by the plant for growth and energy.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'Choice D is the best answer. The combined sentence is grammatically correct and maintains the meaning of the original passage.  The other choices contain errors in punctuation or comma use.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The passage below is adapted from a science textbook discussing the process of photosynthesis. \begin{align*} \text{Carbon dioxide} + \text{Water} + \text{Sunlight} \rightarrow \text{Sugar} + \text{Oxygen} \end{align*} This equation shows that the process of photosynthesis, which plants use to create their own food, results in the production of sugar and oxygen. The sugar is used by the plant for growth and energy, while the oxygen is released into the atmosphere.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '9ac321de',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best describes the main idea of the article?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The author of the article suggests that the current economic climate is characterized by both opportunity and uncertainty.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The author of the article argues that the increase in online shopping is a sign of the economic uncertainty that consumers are facing.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The author of the article suggests that the rise of e-commerce is an example of the new opportunities that businesses are facing in the current economic climate.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The author of the article concludes by arguing that businesses need to be aware of both the opportunities and the challenges of the new economic climate in order to succeed.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The main idea of the article is that the current economic climate is characterized by both opportunity and uncertainty. The author discusses both the uncertainty associated with online shopping and the opportunities presented by e-commerce.  Therefore, A is the best choice.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author of the article suggests that the current economic climate is characterized by both opportunity and uncertainty.  One of the main points is that the rise in the popularity of online shopping is an example of this uncertainty. The author explains that consumers are constantly trying to predict how prices will fluctuate, and that this leads to a great deal of anxiety.  Another of the author’s main points is that the increase in the use of e-commerce is an example of the opportunities presented by the new economic climate. The author suggests that the ability of businesses to reach a wider audience through online platforms is a major advantage that is helping to drive economic growth. The author concludes the article by arguing that businesses need to be aware of both the opportunities and the challenges of the new economic climate in order to succeed.  Which of the following choices best describes the main idea of the article?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_a8',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    '______.  They are particularly interested in how music, like language, can help to strengthen the brain’s ability to detect and process patterns.',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"They are particularly interested in how music, like language, can help to strengthen the brain’s ability to detect and process patterns.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"They are particularly interested in how music, like language, can help to strengthen the brain’s ability to detect and process patterns;","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"They are particularly interested in how music, like language, can help to strengthen the brain’s ability to detect and process patterns.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"They are particularly interested in how music, like language, can help to strengthen the brain’s ability to detect and process patterns—","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The sentence contains two independent clauses, so a comma and a coordinating conjunction are needed to connect them. Choices B, C, and D are incorrect because they use incorrect punctuation to connect the clauses.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'A recent study by researchers at the University of Oxford found that people who regularly listen to music are better at recognizing patterns.  This finding could have important implications for the development of new learning tools, as well as for our understanding of how the brain processes information. The researchers believe that music, like language, can help to strengthen the brain''s ability to detect and process patterns. Which of the following choices most logically completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'random_id_a1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The text is organized as',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"a comparison and contrast of two different types of scrapbooks.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"a general statement followed by a specific example.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"an explanation of a process followed by an analysis of its effects.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"a chronological account of a specific event.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The text begins with a general statement about a common practice of nineteenth-century American women and then focuses on the work of one specific woman, Hannah Ditzler Alspaugh, who is a notable example of this common practice.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The text begins by describing a common practice of nineteenth-century American women. Then, it focuses on the work of one specific woman, Hannah Ditzler Alspaugh, who is a notable example of this common practice.  The text concludes with an explanation of why Alspaugh’s work is considered significant by historians. What is the organizational pattern of the text?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    '849fb8d7',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The sentence below contains a grammatical error. Which choice corrects the error in the sentence?  
 The most obvious sign that the speaker is speaking directly to the reader is the use of the word "you."  There are other elements of the passage that may help to make the speaker seem more immediate and personal. The speaker refers to events and places that are familiar to the reader, and the speaker uses a conversational tone throughout the passage.',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The most obvious sign that the speaker is speaking directly to the reader is the use of the word \"you.\" There are other elements of the passage that may help to make the speaker seem more immediate and personal. The speaker refers to events and places that are familiar to the reader, and the speaker uses a conversational tone throughout the passage.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The most obvious sign that the speaker is speaking directly to the reader, is the use of the word \"you.\" There are other elements of the passage that may help to make the speaker seem more immediate and personal. The speaker refers to events and places that are familiar to the reader, and the speaker uses a conversational tone throughout the passage.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The most obvious sign that the speaker is speaking directly to the reader, is the use of the word \"you.\" There are other elements of the passage that may help to make the speaker seem more immediate and personal; the speaker refers to events and places that are familiar to the reader, and the speaker uses a conversational tone throughout the passage.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The most obvious sign that the speaker is speaking directly to the reader is the use of the word \"you.\" There are other elements of the passage that may help to make the speaker seem more immediate and personal. The speaker refers to events and places that are familiar to the reader, and uses a conversational tone throughout the passage.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The original sentence contains a comma splice because two independent clauses are incorrectly joined with only a comma.  Choice D corrects this by removing the comma and replacing it with a period, which correctly marks the boundary between the two sentences.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    NULL,
    NULL,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '987bd4f1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The question at the beginning of this passage is most likely included to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"highlight the challenge that the students are facing.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"introduce the topic of the passage and engage the reader.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"provide context for the students’ learning experience.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"suggest that the students are struggling with the skill.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The question introduces the topic of the passage: the students’ learning experience. The question also engages the reader by making them think about the topic. This helps the reader to be more interested in reading the rest of the passage.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The text describes a group of students who are being taught how to perform a particular skill, and the text emphasizes that the students are learning a new skill. The text also emphasizes the importance of the skill in the wider world. What is the most likely reason the writer chooses to begin the passage with a question?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'random_id_e3',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best describes the effect of the author''s use of figurative language in this sentence?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The figurative language makes the sentence sound more dramatic and powerful.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The figurative language emphasizes the character’s feelings of hope and optimism.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The figurative language reveals the character’s lack of understanding of the world.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The figurative language is used to create a sense of humor and irony.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The figurative language, "a handful of dust," is a powerful and evocative image that emphasizes the character''s feeling of despair and loss. It creates a sense of tragedy and emphasizes the finality of the character''s situation.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author of the novel "A Handful of Dust" describes a scene in which a character, Tony Last, is forced to make a difficult decision:  "Last was a man who knew, to the marrow of his being, that he had done everything he could do to make the world a better place. He had given his heart and soul to the task. But now, the world seemed to be slipping away from him, and he was left with nothing but a handful of dust."  What is the effect of the author''s use of figurative language in this sentence?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'random_id_english',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this passage, the author likely mentions that the two sisters are very different in order to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"illustrate the importance of family and support.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"emphasize the contrast between the two sisters'' personalities.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"explain how the sisters'' differences lead to conflict.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"show how the sisters'' differences make their bond even stronger.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,

    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The novel follows the lives of two sisters, one named Amelia and the other named Beatrice.  Amelia is a quiet and introspective person who loves to read, while Beatrice is outgoing and adventurous.  The sisters are very different, but they are also very close.  When Amelia is struggling, Beatrice is always there to support her.  And when Beatrice is feeling lost, Amelia is always there to offer her a listening ear.  They have a unique bond that helps them through life''s challenges. What is the most likely reason the author mentions that the two sisters are very different?',

    'It had been a long time since they had seen one another.  The two friends had grown apart over the years, and the once-close bond that they shared had faded.     'A',
    'The study only shows a correlation between social media use and mental well-being, but it does not establish a causal relationship. It is possible that people who are already struggling with mental health issues are more likely to spend more time on social media, rather than the other way around. To establish a causal link, the researchers would need to conduct a study that controls for other factors that could be contributing to mental health issues.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'A new study has been published in the journal *Nature* that claims that the use of social media is linked to a decline in mental well-being. The study tracked the amount of time that a sample of 500 people spent using social media each day and correlated that data to their levels of anxiety and depression. The researchers found that people who spent more than two hours a day using social media had a higher likelihood of reporting anxiety and depression symptoms compared to those who spent less than two hours a day on social media.',

    'Which of the following inferences is BEST supported by the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Giraffes are not as silent as scientists once thought.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The low-pitched humming sound used by giraffes in European zoos is a result of the giraffes’ adaptation to their environment.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The study by Stöger and her team conclusively proves that giraffes do not always communicate visually.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Giraffes in the wild are not as silent as giraffes in European zoos.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The text states that the researchers believe giraffes use the humming sound when visual communication is impossible. This implies that the low-pitched humming sound is an adaptation made by the giraffes to their environment. The passage does not provide information to support the other choices.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author of the following text discusses a group of animals’ adaptation to a challenging environment. The text also highlights the fact that humans are not always the cause of such adaptations.  

Many scientists have long believed that giraffes are mostly silent and communicate only visually with one another. But biologist Angela Stöger and her team analyzed hundreds of hours of recordings of giraffes in three European zoos and found that giraffes make a very low-pitched humming sound. The researchers claim that the giraffes use these sounds to communicate when it’s not possible for them to signal one another visually. This finding, they say, could help to explain why giraffes have such a low-pitched humming sound, which the team claims is used by giraffes in three European zoos, and that giraffes may not be as silent as they are often thought.',
